title: Human Attribution of Causality to AI Across Agency, Misuse, and Misalignment
summary: This study investigates how humans attribute causal responsibility in AI-related incidents, finding that people assign greater blame to AI systems with higher autonomy. The research shows that developers are judged as highly causal despite temporal distance, which has important implications for establishing liability frameworks and shaping policy responses to AI-related harms.
content:
arXiv:2603.13236v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI-related incidents are becoming increasingly frequent and severe, ranging from safety failures to misuse by malicious actors. In such complex situations, identifying which elements caused an adverse outcome, the problem of cause selection, is a critical first step for establishing liability. This paper investigates folk perceptions of causal responsibility in causal chain structures when AI systems are involved in harmful outcomes. We conduct human experiments to examine judgments of causality, blame, foreseeability, and counterfactual reasoning. Our findings show that: (1) When AI agency was moderate (human sets the goal, AI determines the means) or high (AI sets the goal and the means), participants attributed greater causal responsibility to the AI. However, under low AI agency (where a human sets both a goal and means) participants assigned greater causal responsibility to the human despite their temporal distance from the outcome and despite both agents intended it, suggesting an effect of autonomy; (2) When we reversed roles between human and AI, participants consistently judged the human as more causal, even when both agents perform the same action; (3) The developer, despite being distant in the chain, was judged highly causal, reducing causal attributions to the human user but not to the AI; (4) Decomposing the AI into a large language model and an agentic component showed that the agentic part was judged as more causal in the chain. Overall, our research provides evidence on how people perceive the causal contribution of AI in both misuse and misalignment scenarios, and how these judgments interact with the roles of users and developers, key actors in assigning responsibility. These findings can inform the design of liability frameworks for AI-caused harms and shed light on how intuitive judgments shape social and policy debates surrounding real-world AI-related incidents.

zh.title: 人类对AI因果归因的研究：涵盖自主性、滥用和错配
zh.summary: 本研究调查了人类在AI相关事件中如何归因因果责任，发现人们对自主性更高的AI系统分配更大的责任。研究表明，尽管时间距离较远，开发者仍被判定为具有高度因果性，这对建立责任框架和制定AI相关伤害的政策应对具有重要意义。

fr.title: Attribution humaine de la causalité à l'IA selon l'agentivité, l'abus et la désalignement
fr.summary: Cette étude examine comment les humains attribuent la responsabilité causale dans les incidents liés à l'IA, découvrant que les gens attribuent un plus grand blâme aux systèmes d'IA avec une autonomie plus élevée. La recherche montre que les développeurs sont jugés hautement causals malgré la distance temporelle, ce qui a des implications importantes pour l'établissement de cadres de responsabilité et la façon de façonner les réponses politiques aux préjudices liés à l'IA.

de.title: Menschliche Zuschreibung von Kausalität zu KI über Handlungsfähigkeit, Missbrauch und Fehlausrichtung
de.summary: Diese Studie untersucht, wie Menschen in KI-bezogenen Vorfällen Kausalverantwortung zuschreiben, und stellt fest, dass Menschen KI-Systemen mit höherer Autonomie eine höhere Schuld zuweisen. Die Forschung zeigt, dass Entwickler trotz zeitlichen Abstands als hochgradig kausal beurteilt werden, was wichtige Auswirkungen auf die Festlegung von Haftungsrahmenbedingungen und die Gestaltung von politischen Reaktionen auf KI-bezogene Schäden hat.

es.title: Atribución humana de causalidad a la IA en agencia, abuso y desalineación
es.summary: Este estudio investiga cómo los humanos atribuyen responsabilidad causal en incidentes relacionados con la IA, descubriendo que las personas asignan mayor culpa a sistemas de IA con mayor autonomía. La investigación muestra que los desarrolladores se juzgan como altamente causales a pesar de la distancia temporal, lo que tiene implicaciones importantes para establecer marcos de responsabilidad y dar forma a las respuestas políticas a los daños relacionados con la IA.

title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
summary: This paper proposes a dual-path generative framework combining VAE and WGAN-GP to detect zero-day banking fraud while maintaining sub-50ms latency and regulatory explainability. The system addresses extreme class imbalance by synthesizing adversarial scenarios and using selective SHAP-based explanations to reconcile real-time performance with GDPR compliance requirements.
content:
arXiv:2603.13237v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: High-frequency banking environments face a critical trade-off between low-latency fraud detection and the regulatory explainability demanded by GDPR. Traditional rule-based and discriminative models struggle with "zero-day" attacks due to extreme class imbalance and the lack of historical precedents. This paper proposes a Dual-Path Generative Framework that decouples real-time anomaly detection from offline adversarial training. The architecture employs a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) to establish a legitimate transaction manifold based on reconstruction error, ensuring <50ms inference latency. In parallel, an asynchronous Wasserstein GAN with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) synthesizes high-entropy fraudulent scenarios to stress-test the detection boundaries. Crucially, to address the non-differentiability of discrete banking data (e.g., Merchant Category Codes), we integrate a Gumbel-Softmax estimator. Furthermore, we introduce a trigger-based explainability mechanism where SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) is activated only for high-uncertainty transactions, reconciling the computational cost of XAI with real-time throughput requirements.

zh.title: 用于银行系统零日欺诈检测的双路径生成框架
zh.summary: 本文提出了一个双路径生成框架，结合VAE和WGAN-GP来检测零日银行欺诈，同时保持50毫秒以下的延迟和监管可解释性。该系统通过合成对抗场景并使用选择性SHAP解释来处理极端类不平衡，以协调实时性能与GDPR合规要求。

fr.title: Un cadre génératif à double voie pour la détection de fraude zéro-jour dans les systèmes bancaires
fr.summary: Cet article propose un cadre génératif à double voie combinant VAE et WGAN-GP pour détecter les fraudes bancaires zéro-jour tout en maintenant une latence inférieure à 50ms et une explicabilité réglementaire. Le système aborde le déséquilibre des classes extrême en synthétisant des scénarios adversariaux et en utilisant des explications sélectives basées sur SHAP pour concilier les performances en temps réel avec les exigences de conformité GDPR.

de.title: Ein Dual-Path-Generativrahmen zur Erkennung von Zero-Day-Betrug in Bankensystemen
de.summary: Diese Arbeit schlägt einen Dual-Path-Generativrahmen vor, der VAE und WGAN-GP kombiniert, um Zero-Day-Bankbetrug zu erkennen und gleichzeitig eine Latenz unter 50 ms und behördliche Erklärbarkeit zu gewährleisten. Das System befasst sich mit extremem Klassenungleichgewicht durch die Synthese von gegnerischen Szenarien und die Verwendung von selektiven SHAP-basierten Erklärungen, um die Echtzeitleistung mit GDPR-Compliance-Anforderungen in Einklang zu bringen.

es.title: Un marco generativo de doble ruta para la detección de fraude de día cero en sistemas bancarios
es.summary: Este documento propone un marco generativo de doble ruta que combina VAE y WGAN-GP para detectar fraude bancario de día cero mientras se mantiene una latencia inferior a 50 ms y explicabilidad regulatoria. El sistema aborda el desequilibrio de clases extremo sintetizando escenarios adversarios y usando explicaciones selectivas basadas en SHAP para reconciliar el desempeño en tiempo real con los requisitos de cumplimiento GDPR.

title: Benchmarking Zero-Shot Reasoning Approaches for Error Detection in Solidity Smart Contracts
summary: This paper benchmarks state-of-the-art LLMs on detecting vulnerabilities in Solidity smart contracts using zero-shot reasoning strategies, finding that Chain-of-Thought and Tree-of-Thought prompting substantially improve recall but increase false positives. The results establish effectiveness baselines for using LLMs to automate smart contract security analysis, addressing critical financial risks.
content:
arXiv:2603.13239v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Smart contracts play a central role in blockchain systems by encoding financial and operational logic. Still, their susceptibility to subtle security flaws poses significant risks of financial loss and erosion of trust. LLMs create new opportunities for automating vulnerability detection, yet the effectiveness of different prompting strategies and model choices in real-world contexts remains uncertain. This paper evaluates state-of-the-art LLMs on Solidity smart contract analysis using a balanced dataset of 400 contracts under two tasks: (i) Error Detection, where the model performs binary classification to decide whether a contract is vulnerable, and (ii) Error Classification, where the model must assign the predicted issue to a specific vulnerability category. Models are evaluated using zero-shot prompting strategies, including zero-shot, zero-shot Chain-of-Thought (CoT), and zero-shot Tree-of-Thought (ToT). In the Error Detection task, CoT and ToT substantially increase recall (often approaching $\approx 95$--$99\%$), but typically reduce precision, indicating a more sensitive decision regime with more false positives. In the Error Classification task, Claude 3 Opus attains the best Weighted F1-score (90.8) under the ToT prompt, followed closely by its CoT.

zh.title: Solidity智能合约错误检测中零样本推理方法的基准测试
zh.summary: 本文使用零样本推理策略对最先进的大语言模型在Solidity智能合约漏洞检测中的表现进行了基准测试，发现思维链和思维树提示大幅提高了召回率但增加了假阳性。这些结果为使用大语言模型自动化智能合约安全分析建立了有效性基线，解决了关键的金融风险。

fr.title: Benchmarking des approches de raisonnement zéro-shot pour la détection d'erreurs dans les contrats intelligents Solidity
fr.summary: Cet article évalue les modèles de langage de pointe sur la détection des vulnérabilités dans les contrats intelligents Solidity en utilisant des stratégies de raisonnement zéro-shot, découvrant que le chaînage de la pensée et l'incitation à l'arbre de la pensée améliorent considérablement le rappel mais augmentent les faux positifs. Les résultats établissent des lignes de base d'efficacité pour l'utilisation de modèles de langage pour automatiser l'analyse de la sécurité des contrats intelligents, abordant les risques financiers critiques.

de.title: Benchmarking von Zero-Shot-Reasoning-Ansätzen zur Fehlererkennung in Solidity Smart Contracts
de.summary: Dieses Papier bewertet hochmoderne LLMs bei der Erkennung von Schwachstellen in Solidity Smart Contracts mithilfe von Zero-Shot-Reasoning-Strategien und stellt fest, dass Chain-of-Thought- und Tree-of-Thought-Prompts den Recall erheblich verbessern, aber falsch-positive Ergebnisse erhöhen. Die Ergebnisse etablieren Effektivitätsbaselines für die Verwendung von LLMs zur Automatisierung der Smart-Contract-Sicherheitsanalyse und behandeln kritische finanzielle Risiken.

es.title: Evaluación de enfoques de razonamiento cero para la detección de errores en contratos inteligentes Solidity
es.summary: Este documento evalúa modelos de lenguaje de última generación en la detección de vulnerabilidades en contratos inteligentes Solidity utilizando estrategias de razonamiento cero, encontrando que el encadenamiento de pensamiento y el mensaje de árbol de pensamiento mejoran sustancialmente el recuerdo pero aumentan los falsos positivos. Los resultados establecen líneas de base de efectividad para usar modelos de lenguaje para automatizar el análisis de seguridad de contratos inteligentes, abordando riesgos financieros críticos.

title: Think First, Diffuse Fast: Improving Diffusion Language Model Reasoning via Autoregressive Plan Conditioning
summary: This work introduces plan conditioning, a training-free method that prepends a natural-language plan from an autoregressive model to improve diffusion language models' multi-step reasoning. On GSM8K and HumanEval, plan conditioning delivers dramatic gains (11.6-12.8 percentage points), enabling diffusion models to match autoregressive baselines while maintaining inference stability advantages.
content:
arXiv:2603.13243v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) generate text via iterative denoising but consistently underperform on multi-step reasoning. We hypothesize this gap stems from a coordination problem: AR models build coherence token-by-token, while diffusion models must coordinate all positions simultaneously. We propose plan conditioning, a training-free method that prepends a short (~100-token) natural-language plan from an AR model to the diffusion model's prompt. The plan serves as a frozen scaffold -- globally visible context that every token position can attend to from the first denoising step. On GSM8K, plan conditioning improves LLaDA-8B-Instruct from 75.6% to 87.2% (+11.6 percentage points), matching a same-size AR model (LLaMA 3.1 8B, 87.7%) despite a 6.4pp weaker baseline. On HumanEval, the gain is +12.8pp (37.2% to 50.0%), showing plans generalize to code. The same plans improve LLaMA by only +5.7pp on GSM8K and +1.3pp on HumanEval -- diffusion models benefit 2-10x more, supporting the coordination-problem hypothesis. Across 5 random seeds, plan-conditioned GSM8K accuracy has zero standard deviation, making diffusion inference highly stable. Ablations reveal the model follows plan strategy (wrong-strategy plans cause -16.3pp) but is robust to plan values (perturbed numbers: -1.1pp), and that planner quality has a sharp threshold: smaller Llama-class plans hurt (-1.6 to -6.8pp) while frontier plans provide the full lift. Attention analysis confirms the mechanism: plan tokens receive 1.8x excess attention during early denoising, declining to uniform as completion tokens solidify. Plan conditioning costs ~$0.002 per problem and adds ~2s of latency.

zh.title: 先思考后扩散：通过自回归计划条件改进扩散语言模型推理
zh.summary: 这项工作引入了计划条件化，一种无需训练的方法，它从自回归模型前置自然语言计划来改进扩散语言模型的多步推理。在GSM8K和HumanEval上，计划条件化实现了显著的收益（提升11.6-12.8个百分点），使扩散模型能够与自回归基线相匹配，同时保持推理稳定性优势。

fr.title: Penser d'abord, diffuser vite : Améliorer le raisonnement du modèle de langage par diffusion via le conditionnement du plan autoregressif
fr.summary: Ce travail introduit le conditionnement du plan, une méthode sans entraînement qui prépend un plan en langage naturel d'un modèle autoregressif pour améliorer le raisonnement multi-étapes des modèles de langage par diffusion. Sur GSM8K et HumanEval, le conditionnement du plan apporte des gains spectaculaires (11,6-12,8 points de pourcentage), permettant aux modèles de diffusion d'égaler les bases de référence autoregressives tout en maintenant les avantages de stabilité de l'inférence.

de.title: Erst denken, schnell diffundieren: Verbesserung des Diffusions-Sprachmodell-Reasonings durch autoregressive Plan-Konditionierung
de.summary: Diese Arbeit führt Plan-Konditionierung ein, eine trainingsfreie Methode, die einen natürlichsprachigen Plan aus einem autoregressiven Modell voranstellt, um das mehrstufige Reasoning von Diffusions-Sprachmodellen zu verbessern. Bei GSM8K und HumanEval erreicht die Plan-Konditionierung dramatische Gewinne (11,6-12,8 Prozentpunkte) und ermöglicht es Diffusionsmodellen, autoregressive Baselines zu erreichen und gleichzeitig Vorteile der Inferenz-Stabilität zu bewahren.

es.title: Pensar primero, difundir rápido: Mejora del razonamiento del modelo de lenguaje por difusión mediante acondicionamiento de plan autorregresivo
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce el acondicionamiento de plan, un método sin entrenamiento que antepone un plan en lenguaje natural de un modelo autorregresivo para mejorar el razonamiento multi-paso de modelos de lenguaje por difusión. En GSM8K y HumanEval, el acondicionamiento de plan ofrece ganancias dramáticas (11,6-12,8 puntos porcentuales), permitiendo que los modelos de difusión coincidan con líneas base autoregressivas mientras mantienen ventajas de estabilidad de inferencia.

title: Automating Document Intelligence in Statutory City Planning
summary: This paper presents an AI system deployed at UK local planning authorities to automate compliance-heavy document processing while respecting privacy regulations. The system uses an AI-in-the-loop approach where all suggestions require human approval, combining automatic PII redaction, metadata extraction, and drawing analysis to reduce administrative burden and manage legal compliance risk.
content:
arXiv:2603.13245v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: UK planning authorities face a legislative conflict between the Planning Act, which mandates public access to application documents, and the Data Protection Act, which requires protection of personal information. This situation creates a manually intensive workload for processing large document volumes, diverting planning officers to administrative tasks and creating legal compliance risks. This paper presents an integrated AI system designed to address these challenges. The system automates the identification and redaction of personal information, extracts key metadata from planning documents, and analyzes architectural drawings for specified features. It operates with an AI-in-the-Loop (AI2L) design, presenting all suggestions for review and confirmation by planning officers directly within their existing software; no action is committed without explicit human approval. The system is designed to improve its performance over time by learning from this human oversight through active learning prioritization rather than autoapproval. The system is currently being piloted at four diverse UK local authorities. The paper details the system design, the AI2L workflow, and the evaluation framework used in the pilot. Additionally, it describes a preliminary Return on Investment (ROI) model developed to quantify potential savings and secure partner participation. This work provides a case study on deploying AI to reduce administrative burden and manage compliance risk in a public sector environment.

zh.title: 自动化法定城市规划中的文档智能处理
zh.summary: 本论文介绍了一个AI系统，部署在英国当地规划机构中，用于自动化符合性强的文档处理，同时尊重隐私法规。该系统采用人工智能在环的方法，其中所有建议都需要人类批准，结合自动PII编辑、元数据提取和图形分析，以减少行政负担并管理法律合规风险。

fr.title: Automatisation de l'intelligence documentaire dans la planification urbaine statutaire
fr.summary: Cet article présente un système d'IA déployé auprès des autorités de planification locales du Royaume-Uni pour automatiser le traitement de documents lourd en conformité tout en respectant la réglementation sur la confidentialité. Le système utilise une approche homme-IA où toutes les suggestions nécessitent l'approbation humaine, combinant la rédaction automatique des PII, l'extraction de métadonnées et l'analyse des dessins pour réduire la charge administrative et gérer les risques de conformité juridique.

de.title: Automatisierung der Dokumentintelligenz in der gesetzlichen Stadtplanung
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert ein KI-System, das bei lokalen Planungsbehörden in Großbritannien eingesetzt wird, um die sperrende Dokumentenverarbeitung zu automatisieren und gleichzeitig Datenschutzbestimmungen zu beachten. Das System nutzt einen Mensch-KI-in-der-Schleife-Ansatz, bei dem alle Vorschläge menschliche Genehmigung erfordern, und kombiniert automatische PII-Schwärzung, Metadatenextraction und Zeichnungsanalyse, um den Verwaltungsaufwand zu verringern und Rechtskonformitätsrisiken zu bewältigen.

es.title: Automatización de inteligencia de documentos en planificación urbana estatutaria
es.summary: Este artículo presenta un sistema de IA implementado en autoridades de planificación local en el Reino Unido para automatizar el procesamiento de documentos de cumplimiento normativo mientras respeta la regulación de privacidad. El sistema utiliza un enfoque de IA en el bucle donde todas las sugerencias requieren aprobación humana, combinando redacción automática de PII, extracción de metadatos y análisis de dibujos para reducir la carga administrativa y gestionar el riesgo de cumplimiento legal.

title: Multi-Axis Trust Modeling for Interpretable Account Hijacking Detection
summary: This paper proposes a multi-axis trust modeling framework inspired by classical Hadith scholarship that translates five interpretable trust dimensions into behavioral features for account hijacking detection. The approach substantially outperforms traditional anomaly detection and statistical baselines, with temporal features providing significant improvements on highly imbalanced security datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.13246v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper proposes a Hadith-inspired multi-axis trust modeling framework, motivated by a structurally analogous problem in classical Hadith scholarship: assessing the trustworthiness of information sources using interpretable, multidimensional criteria rather than a single anomaly score. We translate five trust axes - long-term integrity (adalah), behavioral precision (dabt), contextual continuity (isnad), cumulative reputation, and anomaly evidence - into a compact set of 26 semantically meaningful behavioral features for user accounts. In addition, we introduce lightweight temporal features that capture short-horizon changes in these trust signals across consecutive activity windows. We evaluate the framework on the CLUE-LDS cloud activity dataset with injected account hijacking scenarios. On 23,094 sliding windows, a Random Forest trained on the trust features achieves near-perfect detection performance, substantially outperforming models based on raw event counts, minimal statistical baselines, and unsupervised anomaly detection. Temporal features provide modest but consistent gains on CLUE-LDS, confirming their compatibility with the static trust representation. To assess robustness under more challenging conditions, we further evaluate the approach on the CERT Insider Threat Test Dataset r6.2, which exhibits extreme class imbalance and sparse malicious behavior. On a 500-user CERT subset, temporal features improve ROC-AUC from 0.776 to 0.844. On a leakage-controlled 4,000-user configuration, temporal modeling yields a substantial and consistent improvement over static trust features alone (ROC-AUC 0.627 to 0.715; PR-AUC 0.072 to 0.264).

zh.title: 多轴信任建模用于可解释的账户劫持检测
zh.summary: 本论文提出了一个受古典圣训学启发的多轴信任建模框架，将五个可解释的信任维度转化为用于账户劫持检测的行为特征。该方法在传统异常检测和统计基线方面表现出色，时间特征在高度不平衡的安全数据集上提供了显著的改进。

fr.title: Modélisation multi-axes de la confiance pour la détection d'usurpation de compte interprétable
fr.summary: Cet article propose un cadre de modélisation multi-axes de la confiance inspiré par les études classiques du Hadith qui traduit cinq dimensions de confiance interprétables en caractéristiques comportementales pour la détection d'usurpation de compte. L'approche surpasse considérablement la détection d'anomalies traditionnelle et les approches statistiques de base, les caractéristiques temporelles apportant des améliorations significatives sur les ensembles de données de sécurité hautement déséquilibrés.

de.title: Multi-Achsen-Vertrauensmodellierung für interpretierbare Kontoübernahme-Erkennung
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt ein Multi-Achsen-Vertrauensmodellierungs-Framework vor, inspiriert von klassischen Hadith-Studien, das fünf interpretierbare Vertrauensdimensionen in Verhaltensmerkmale für die Kontoübernahme-Erkennung übersetzt. Der Ansatz übertrifft deutlich die traditionelle Anomalieerkennung und statistische Baselines, wobei zeitliche Merkmale erhebliche Verbesserungen bei hochgradig unausgeglichenen Sicherheitsdatensätzen bieten.

es.title: Modelado de confianza multi-eje para detección interpretable de secuestro de cuenta
es.summary: Este artículo propone un marco de modelado de confianza multi-eje inspirado en estudios clásicos de Hadith que traduce cinco dimensiones de confianza interpretables en características conductuales para la detección de secuestro de cuenta. El enfoque supera sustancialmente la detección tradicional de anomalías y las líneas base estadísticas, con características temporales proporcionando mejoras significativas en conjuntos de datos de seguridad altamente desequilibrados.

title: ILION: Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates for Agentic AI Systems
summary: ILION is a deterministic safety gate for autonomous AI agents that evaluates proposed actions without statistical training or API dependencies, operating at sub-millisecond latency with 91% precision. The system outperforms existing content-moderation infrastructure by 4.3 F1 points while operating 2,000x faster, addressing a critical safety gap for AI systems capable of real-world action.
content:
arXiv:2603.13247v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The proliferation of autonomous AI agents capable of executing real-world actions - filesystem operations, API calls, database modifications, financial transactions - introduces a class of safety risk not addressed by existing content-moderation infrastructure. Current text-safety systems evaluate linguistic content for harm categories such as violence, hate speech, and sexual content; they are architecturally unsuitable for evaluating whether a proposed action falls within an agent's authorized operational scope. We present ILION (Intelligent Logic Identity Operations Network), a deterministic execution gate for agentic AI systems. ILION employs a five-component cascade architecture - Transient Identity Imprint (TII), Semantic Vector Reference Frame (SVRF), Identity Drift Control (IDC), Identity Resonance Score (IRS) and Consensus Veto Layer (CVL) - to classify proposed agent actions as BLOCK or ALLOW without statistical training or API dependencies. The system requires zero labeled data, operates in sub-millisecond latency, and produces fully interpretable verdicts. We evaluate ILION on ILION-Bench v2, a purpose-built benchmark of 380 test scenarios across eight attack categories with 39% hard-difficulty adversarial cases and a held-out development split. ILION achieves F1 = 0.8515, precision = 91.0%, and a false positive rate of 7.9% at a mean latency of 143 microseconds. Comparative evaluation against three baselines - Lakera Guard (F1 = 0.8087), OpenAI Moderation API (F1 = 0.1188), and Llama Guard 3 (F1 = 0.0105) - demonstrates that existing text-safety infrastructure systematically fails on agent execution safety tasks due to a fundamental task mismatch. ILION outperforms the best commercial baseline by 4.3 F1 points while operating 2,000 times faster with a false positive rate four times lower.

zh.title: ILION: 代理AI系统的确定性前置执行安全门控
zh.summary: ILION是一个用于自主AI代理的确定性安全门控，可以评估提议的行动，无需统计训练或API依赖，在亚毫秒级延迟下运行，精确度达到91%。该系统在现有内容审核基础设施上表现超出4.3 F1点，同时运行速度快2000倍，为能够执行真实世界行动的AI系统解决了关键安全缺口。

fr.title: ILION: Portes de sécurité de pré-exécution déterministes pour les systèmes d'IA agentiques
fr.summary: ILION est une porte de sécurité déterministe pour les agents d'IA autonomes qui évalue les actions proposées sans formation statistique ou dépendances d'API, fonctionnant à une latence sub-milliseconde avec une précision de 91%. Le système surpasse l'infrastructure de modération de contenu existante de 4,3 points F1 tout en fonctionnant 2000 fois plus vite, comblant une lacune de sécurité critique pour les systèmes d'IA capables d'actions du monde réel.

de.title: ILION: Deterministische Sicherheitstore vor der Ausführung für agentische KI-Systeme
de.summary: ILION ist ein deterministisches Sicherheitstor für autonome KI-Agenten, das vorgeschlagene Aktionen ohne statistische Schulung oder API-Abhängigkeiten bewertet und mit einer Sub-Millisekunden-Latenz und einer Genauigkeit von 91% arbeitet. Das System übertrifft die vorhandene Inhaltsmoderationsinfrastruktur um 4,3 F1-Punkte und arbeitet 2000-mal schneller, was eine kritische Sicherheitslücke für KI-Systeme schließt, die zu realen Aktionen fähig sind.

es.title: ILION: Puertas de seguridad deterministas de pre-ejecución para sistemas de IA agentiva
es.summary: ILION es una puerta de seguridad determinista para agentes de IA autónomos que evalúa acciones propuestas sin entrenamiento estadístico ni dependencias de API, operando a una latencia sub-milisegunda con una precisión del 91%. El sistema supera la infraestructura de moderación de contenido existente por 4,3 puntos F1 mientras opera 2000 veces más rápido, abordando una brecha de seguridad crítica para sistemas de IA capaces de acciones en el mundo real.

title: ManiBench: A Benchmark for Testing Visual-Logic Drift and Syntactic Hallucinations in Manim Code Generation
summary: ManiBench is a specialized benchmark for evaluating LLM performance on Manim animation code generation, addressing failure modes that traditional code benchmarks miss: syntactic hallucinations referencing deprecated APIs and visual-logic drift where generated animations diverge from intended mathematical logic. The benchmark comprises 150-200 problems grounded in analysis of 3Blue1Brown's source code.
content:
arXiv:2603.13251v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Traditional benchmarks like HumanEval and MBPP test logic and syntax effectively, but fail when code must produce dynamic, pedagogical visuals. We introduce ManiBench, a specialized benchmark evaluating LLM performance in generating Manim CE code, where temporal fidelity and version-aware API correctness are critical. ManiBench targets two key failure modes: Syntactic Hallucinations (valid Python referencing non-existent or deprecated Manim APIs) and Visual-Logic Drift (generated visuals diverging from intended mathematical logic through timing errors or missing causal relationships). The benchmark comprises 150-200 problems across five difficulty levels spanning calculus, linear algebra, probability, topology, and AI, grounded in analysis of 3Blue1Brown's ManimGL source (53,000 lines, 143 scene classes). Evaluation uses a four-tier framework measuring Executability, Version-Conflict Error Rate, Alignment Score, and Coverage Score. An open-source framework automates evaluation across multiple models and prompting strategies. Code, data and benchmark suite are available at https://github.com/nabin2004/ManiBench. and the dataset is hosted on https://huggingface.co/datasets/nabin2004/ManiBench.

zh.title: ManiBench: 用于测试Manim代码生成中视觉逻辑漂移和语法幻觉的基准
zh.summary: ManiBench是一个专门的基准，用于评估LLM在Manim动画代码生成方面的性能，解决传统代码基准遗漏的故障模式：引用已弃用API的语法幻觉和生成的动画与预期数学逻辑不符的视觉逻辑漂移。该基准包括150-200个问题，基于对3Blue1Brown源代码的分析。

fr.title: ManiBench: Un point de référence pour tester la dérive logico-visuelle et les hallucinations syntaxiques dans la génération de code Manim
fr.summary: ManiBench est un point de référence spécialisé pour évaluer les performances des LLM sur la génération de code d'animation Manim, traitant les modes de défaillance que les repères de code traditionnels manquent : les hallucinations syntaxiques référençant des API obsolètes et la dérive logico-visuelle où les animations générées s'écartent de la logique mathématique prévue. L'indice de référence comprend 150-200 problèmes fondés sur l'analyse du code source de 3Blue1Brown.

de.title: ManiBench: Ein Benchmark zum Testen von visuellen Logikdrifts und syntaktischen Halluzinationen bei der Manim-Code-Generierung
de.summary: ManiBench ist ein spezialisiertes Benchmark zur Bewertung der LLM-Leistung bei der Manim-Animationscode-Generierung und behandelt Fehlermodi, die traditionelle Code-Benchmarks übersehen: syntaktische Halluzinationen, die auf veraltete APIs verweisen, und visuelle Logikdrift, bei der generierte Animationen von der beabsichtigten mathematischen Logik abweichen. Das Benchmark umfasst 150-200 Probleme, die auf der Analyse von 3Blue1Browns Quellcode basieren.

es.title: ManiBench: Un punto de referencia para probar la desviación lógico-visual y alucinaciones sintácticas en la generación de código Manim
es.summary: ManiBench es un punto de referencia especializado para evaluar el desempeño de LLM en la generación de código de animación Manim, abordando modos de fallo que los puntos de referencia de código tradicionales pierden: alucinaciones sintácticas que hacen referencia a API obsoletas y desviación lógico-visual donde las animaciones generadas divergen de la lógica matemática prevista. El punto de referencia comprende 150-200 problemas basados en análisis del código fuente de 3Blue1Brown.

title: When Alpha Breaks: Two-Level Uncertainty for Safe Deployment of Cross-Sectional Stock Rankers
summary: This paper develops a two-level uncertainty quantification framework for safe deployment of ML-based stock ranking strategies, using a regime-trust gate to determine whether to trade and position-level risk caps for uncertain predictions. The approach improves risk-adjusted performance by treating deployment as two separate decisions rather than relying solely on point predictions.
content:
arXiv:2603.13252v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Cross-sectional ranking models are often deployed as if point predictions were sufficient: the model outputs scores and the portfolio follows the induced ordering. Under non-stationarity, rankers can fail during regime shifts. In the AI Stock Forecaster, a LightGBM ranker performs well overall at a 20-day horizon, yet the 2024 holdout coincides with an AI thematic rally and sector rotation that breaks the signal at longer horizons and weakens 20d. This motivates treating deployment as two decisions: (i) whether the strategy should trade at all, and (ii) how to control risk within active trades. We adapt Direct Epistemic Uncertainty Prediction (DEUP) to ranking by predicting rank displacement and defining an epistemic uncertainty signal ehat relative to a point-in-time (PIT-safe) baseline. Empirically, ehat is structurally coupled with signal strength (median correlation between ehat and absolute score is about 0.6 across 1,865 dates), so inverse-uncertainty sizing de-levers the strongest signals and degrades performance. To address this, we propose a two-level deployment policy: a strategy-level regime-trust gate G(t) that decides whether to trade (AUROC around 0.72 overall and 0.75 in FINAL) and a position-level epistemic tail-risk cap that reduces exposure only for the most uncertain predictions. The operational policy, trade only when G(t) is at least 0.2, apply volatility sizing on active dates, and cap the top epistemic tail, improves risk-adjusted performance in the 20d policy comparison and indicates DEUP adds value mainly as a tail-risk guard rather than a continuous sizing denominator.

zh.title: 当Alpha破裂时：用于跨截面股票排名器安全部署的两级不确定性
zh.summary: 本文开发了一个两级不确定性量化框架，用于安全部署基于ML的股票排名策略，使用一个体制信任门来确定是否交易，以及为不确定预测设置头寸级风险上限。该方法通过将部署视为两个独立决策而不是仅依赖点预测来改进风险调整后的性能。

fr.title: Quand Alpha casse : Incertitude à deux niveaux pour le déploiement sûr des classeurs boursiers transversaux
fr.summary: Cet article développe un cadre de quantification de l'incertitude à deux niveaux pour le déploiement sûr des stratégies de classement des actions basées sur le ML, en utilisant une porte de confiance de régime pour déterminer s'il faut négocier et des plafonds de risque au niveau de la position pour les prédictions incertaines. L'approche améliore la performance ajustée au risque en traitant le déploiement comme deux décisions séparées plutôt que de se fier uniquement aux prédictions ponctuelles.

de.title: Wenn Alpha bricht: Zwei-Ebenen-Unsicherheit für sichere Bereitstellung von querschnittlichen Aktienranking-Systemen
de.summary: Dieses Papier entwickelt ein zweistufiges Unsicherheitsquantifizierungsframework für die sichere Bereitstellung von ML-basierten Aktienranking-Strategien, wobei ein Regime-Vertrauens-Gate verwendet wird, um zu bestimmen, ob gehandelt werden soll, und Positionsebenen-Risikogrenzen für unsichere Vorhersagen festgelegt werden. Der Ansatz verbessert die risikoadjustierte Leistung, indem er die Bereitstellung als zwei separate Entscheidungen statt nur auf punktuelle Vorhersagen zu verlassen behandelt.

es.title: Cuando Alpha se rompe: Incertidumbre de dos niveles para el despliegue seguro de clasificadores de acciones transversales
es.summary: Este artículo desarrolla un marco de cuantificación de incertidumbre de dos niveles para el despliegue seguro de estrategias de clasificación de acciones basadas en ML, utilizando una puerta de confianza de régimen para determinar si operar y límites de riesgo a nivel de posición para predicciones inciertas. El enfoque mejora el desempeño ajustado por riesgo al tratar el despliegue como dos decisiones separadas en lugar de confiar únicamente en predicciones puntuales.

title: Distilling Deep Reinforcement Learning into Interpretable Fuzzy Rules: An Explainable AI Framework
summary: This work distills opaque deep reinforcement learning policies into interpretable Takagi-Sugeno-Kang fuzzy rules that humans can verify and understand. Achieving 81% behavioral fidelity with rules like 'IF lander drifting left at high altitude THEN apply upward thrust,' the approach enables safe deployment of DRL in safety-critical applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.13257v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agents achieve remarkable performance in continuous control but remain opaque, hindering deployment in safety-critical domains. Existing explainability methods either provide only local insights (SHAP, LIME) or employ over-simplified surrogates failing to capture continuous dynamics (decision trees). This work proposes a Hierarchical Takagi-Sugeno-Kang (TSK) Fuzzy Classifier System (FCS) distilling neural policies into human-readable IF-THEN rules through K-Means clustering for state partitioning and Ridge Regression for local action inference. Three quantifiable metrics are introduced: Fuzzy Rule Activation Density (FRAD) measuring explanation focus, Fuzzy Set Coverage (FSC) validating vocabulary completeness, and Action Space Granularity (ASG) assessing control mode diversity. Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) validates temporal behavioral fidelity. Empirical evaluation on \textit{Lunar Lander(Continuous)} shows the Triangular membership function variant achieves 81.48\% $\pm$ 0.43\% fidelity, outperforming Decision Trees by 21 percentage points. The framework exhibits statistically superior interpretability (FRAD = 0.814 vs. 0.723 for Gaussian, $p < 0.001$) with low MSE (0.0053) and DTW distance (1.05). Extracted rules such as ``IF lander drifting left at high altitude THEN apply upward thrust with rightward correction'' enable human verification, establishing a pathway toward trustworthy autonomous systems.

zh.title: 将深度强化学习蒸馏为可解释的模糊规则：一个可解释人工智能框架
zh.summary: 这项工作将不透明的深度强化学习策略蒸馏为可解释的Takagi-Sugeno-Kang模糊规则，人类可以验证和理解。通过实现81%的行为保真度，使用"如果着陆器在高空向左漂移，则施加向上推力"等规则，该方法使DRL能够安全部署在安全关键应用中。

fr.title: Distiller l'apprentissage par renforcement profond en règles floues interprétables : un cadre d'IA explicable
fr.summary: Ce travail distille les politiques opaques d'apprentissage par renforcement profond en règles floues de Takagi-Sugeno-Kang interprétables que les humains peuvent vérifier et comprendre. En atteignant 81% de fidélité comportementale avec des règles comme "SI le module d'atterrissage dérive à gauche à haute altitude ALORS appliquer une poussée vers le haut", l'approche permet un déploiement sûr du DRL dans les applications critiques pour la sécurité.

de.title: Destillation von Deep Reinforcement Learning in interpretierbare Fuzzy-Regeln: Ein erklärbares KI-Framework
de.summary: Diese Arbeit destilliert undurchsichtige Deep-Reinforcement-Learning-Richtlinien in interpretierbare Takagi-Sugeno-Kang-Fuzzy-Regeln, die Menschen überprüfen und verstehen können. Mit einer Verhaltenstreue von 81% und Regeln wie "WENN Lander treibt in großer Höhe nach links AB DANN Aufwärtskraft anwenden" ermöglicht der Ansatz eine sichere Bereitstellung von DRL in sicherheitskritischen Anwendungen.

es.title: Destilación del aprendizaje por refuerzo profundo en reglas difusas interpretables: un marco de IA explicable
es.summary: Este trabajo destila políticas opacas de aprendizaje por refuerzo profundo en reglas difusas Takagi-Sugeno-Kang interpretables que los humanos pueden verificar y entender. Al lograr una fidelidad de comportamiento del 81% con reglas como "SI el módulo de aterrizaje se desplaza hacia la izquierda a gran altitud ENTONCES aplicar empuje hacia arriba", el enfoque permite el despliegue seguro de DRL en aplicaciones críticas para la seguridad.

title: Deep Convolutional Architectures for EEG Classification: A Comparative Study with Temporal Augmentation and Confidence-Based Voting
summary: This comparative study evaluates deep learning architectures for EEG-based brain-computer interfaces, demonstrating that 3D CNNs jointly modeling spatiotemporal patterns substantially outperform 2D approaches. The paper introduces temporal shift augmentation during training and confidence-based voting at inference to improve prediction stability for event-related potential classification.
content:
arXiv:2603.13261v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) classification plays a key role in brain-computer interface (BCI) systems, yet it remains challenging due to the low signal-to-noise ratio, temporal variability of neural responses, and limited data availability. In this paper, we present a comparative study of deep learning architectures for classifying event-related potentials (ERPs) in EEG signals. The preprocessing pipeline includes bandpass filtering, spatial filtering, and normalization. We design and compare three main pipelines: a 2D convolutional neural network (CNN) using Common Spatial Pattern (CSP), a second 2D CNN trained directly on raw data for a fair comparison, and a 3D CNN that jointly models spatiotemporal representations. To address ERP latency variations, we introduce a temporal shift augmentation strategy during training. At inference time, we employ a confidence-based test-time voting mechanism to improve prediction stability across shifted trials. An experimental evaluation on a stratified five-fold cross-validation protocol demonstrates that while CSP provides a benefit to the 2D architecture, the proposed 3D CNN significantly outperforms both 2D variants in terms of AUC and balanced accuracy. These findings highlight the effectiveness of temporal-aware architectures and augmentation strategies for robust EEG signal classification.

zh.title: 脑电图分类的深度卷积架构：具有时间增强和基于置信度投票的比较研究
zh.summary: 这项比较研究评估了用于基于脑电图的脑机接口的深度学习架构，表明联合建模时空模式的3D CNN明显优于2D方法。该论文在训练过程中引入了时间移位增强，在推理时引入了基于置信度的投票，以提高事件相关电位分类的预测稳定性。

fr.title: Architectures convolutives profondes pour la classification EEG : une étude comparative avec augmentation temporelle et vote basé sur la confiance
fr.summary: Cette étude comparative évalue les architectures d'apprentissage profond pour les interfaces cerveau-ordinateur basées sur l'EEG, démontrant que les CNN 3D modélisant conjointement les modèles spatiotemporels surpassent considérablement les approches 2D. L'article introduit l'augmentation de décalage temporel lors de l'entraînement et le vote basé sur la confiance lors de l'inférence pour améliorer la stabilité de prédiction pour la classification du potentiel lié à l'événement.

de.title: Tiefe Faltungsarchitekturen zur EEG-Klassifizierung: Eine vergleichende Studie mit zeitlicher Augmentation und konfidenzbasiertem Voting
de.summary: Diese vergleichende Studie bewertet Deep-Learning-Architekturen für EEG-basierte Gehirn-Computer-Schnittstellen und zeigt, dass 3D-CNNs, die räumlich-zeitliche Muster gemeinsam modellieren, 2D-Ansätze erheblich übertreffen. Das Papier führt zeitliche Shift-Augmentation während des Trainings und konfidenzbasiertes Voting bei der Inferenz ein, um die Vorhersagestabilität für die Klassifizierung ereignisbezogener Potenziale zu verbessern.

es.title: Arquitecturas de convolución profunda para clasificación de EEG: un estudio comparativo con aumento temporal y votación basada en confianza
es.summary: Este estudio comparativo evalúa arquitecturas de aprendizaje profundo para interfaces cerebro-computadora basadas en EEG, demostrando que las CNN 3D que modelan conjuntamente patrones espaciotemporales superan considerablemente los enfoques 2D. El documento introduce aumento de desplazamiento temporal durante el entrenamiento y votación basada en confianza en la inferencia para mejorar la estabilidad de predicción para la clasificación de potencial relacionado con eventos.

title: Multi-hop Reasoning and Retrieval in Embedding Space: Leveraging Large Language Models with Knowledge
summary: EMBRAG combines LLM reasoning with knowledge graph integration to address hallucination and knowledge staleness, generating multiple logical rules grounded in KGs and reasoning in embedding space guided by graph structure. The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on KG question-answering benchmarks by leveraging symbolic knowledge as a reliable alternative to memorized LLM knowledge.
content:
arXiv:2603.13266v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As large language models (LLMs) continue to grow in size, their abilities to tackle complex tasks have significantly improved. However, issues such as hallucination and the lack of up-to-date knowledge largely remain unresolved. Knowledge graphs (KGs), which serve as symbolic representations of real-world knowledge, offer a reliable source for enhancing reasoning. Integrating KG retrieval into LLMs can therefore strengthen their reasoning by providing dependable knowledge. Nevertheless, due to limited understanding of the underlying knowledge graph, LLMs may struggle with queries that have multiple interpretations. Additionally, the incompleteness and noise within knowledge graphs may result in retrieval failures. To address these challenges, we propose an embedding-based retrieval reasoning framework EMBRAG. In this approach, the model first generates multiple logical rules grounded in knowledge graphs based on the input query. These rules are then applied to reasoning in the embedding space, guided by the knowledge graph, ensuring more robust and accurate reasoning. A reranker model further interprets these rules and refines the results. Extensive experiments on two benchmark KGQA datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves the new state-of-the-art performance in KG reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 嵌入空间中的多跳推理和检索：利用大型语言模型和知识
zh.summary: EMBRAG结合了LLM推理和知识图谱集成来解决幻觉和知识陈旧问题，生成多个以知识图谱为基础的逻辑规则，并在图结构指导下的嵌入空间中进行推理。该方法通过将符号知识作为可靠的替代方案来对抗LLM记忆知识，在知识图谱问答基准上取得了最先进的性能。

fr.title: Raisonnement multi-hop et récupération dans l'espace d'intégration : Exploitation des grands modèles de langage avec la connaissance
fr.summary: EMBRAG combine le raisonnement LLM avec l'intégration de graphes de connaissances pour résoudre les hallucinations et l'obsolescence des connaissances, en générant plusieurs règles logiques ancrées dans les KGs et en raisonnant dans l'espace d'intégration guidé par la structure du graphe. L'approche atteint des performances à la pointe de la technologie sur les benchmarks de réponses aux questions des KG en exploitant les connaissances symboliques comme alternative fiable aux connaissances mémorisées par les LLM.

de.title: Multi-Hop-Reasoning und Abruf im Einbettungsraum: Nutzung großer Sprachmodelle mit Wissen
de.summary: EMBRAG kombiniert LLM-Reasoning mit Knowledge-Graph-Integration, um Halluzinationen und Wissensveraltung zu beheben, generiert mehrere in KGs verankerte logische Regeln und argumentiert im durch Graphstruktur geleiteten Einbettungsraum. Der Ansatz erreicht hochmoderne Leistung bei KG-Frage-Antwort-Benchmarks, indem symbolisches Wissen als zuverlässige Alternative zu memorierten LLM-Wissen genutzt wird.

es.title: Razonamiento multi-salto y recuperación en espacio de incrustación: aprovechamiento de grandes modelos de lenguaje con conocimiento
es.summary: EMBRAG combina razonamiento de LLM con integración de gráficos de conocimiento para abordar alucinaciones y obsolescencia del conocimiento, generando múltiples reglas lógicas basadas en KGs y razonando en espacio de incrustación guiado por la estructura del gráfico. El enfoque logra rendimiento de última generación en puntos de referencia de preguntas-respuestas de KG al aprovechar el conocimiento simbólico como una alternativa confiable al conocimiento memorizado de LLM.

title: Agent-Based User-Adaptive Filtering for Categorized Harassing Communication
summary: This paper proposes an agent-based framework for personalizing content moderation filters based on individual user tolerance levels across harassment categories. Unlike global moderation rules, adaptive agents learn from user feedback to dynamically adjust filtering thresholds, improving both precision and user satisfaction in online social networks.
content:
arXiv:2603.13288v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We propose an agent-based framework for personalized filtering of categorized harassing communication in online social networks. Unlike global moderation systems that apply uniform filtering rules, our approach models user-specific tolerance levels and preferences through adaptive filtering agents. These agents learn from user feedback and dynamically adjust filtering thresholds across multiple harassment categories, including offensive, abusive, and hateful content. We implement and evaluate the framework using supervised classification techniques and simulated user interaction data. Experimental results demonstrate that adaptive agents improve filtering precision and user satisfaction compared to static models. The proposed system illustrates how agent-based personalization can enhance content moderation while preserving user autonomy in digital social environments.

zh.title: 基于代理的用户自适应分类骚扰通信过滤
zh.summary: 本文提出了一个基于代理的框架，用于根据个人用户对不同骚扰类别的容忍度来个性化内容审核过滤器。与全局审核规则不同，自适应代理从用户反馈中学习，动态调整过滤阈值，提高在线社交网络中的精度和用户满意度。

fr.title: Filtrage adaptatif aux utilisateurs basé sur des agents pour les communications de harcèlement catégorisées
fr.summary: Cet article propose un cadre basé sur des agents pour personnaliser les filtres de modération de contenu en fonction des niveaux de tolérance des utilisateurs individuels aux différentes catégories de harcèlement. Contrairement aux règles de modération mondiales, les agents adaptatifs apprennent à partir des commentaires des utilisateurs pour ajuster dynamiquement les seuils de filtrage, améliorant à la fois la précision et la satisfaction des utilisateurs dans les réseaux sociaux en ligne.

de.title: Agenten-basierte benutzeradaptive Filterung für kategorisierte Belästigungskommunikation
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt ein agenten-basiertes Framework vor, um Inhaltsmoderationsfilter basierend auf individuellen Benutzertoleranzstufen für verschiedene Belästigungskategorien zu personalisieren. Im Gegensatz zu globalen Moderationsregeln lernen adaptive Agenten aus Benutzerfeedback, um Filterschwellen dynamisch anzupassen und sowohl die Genauigkeit als auch die Benutzerzufriedenheit in Online-Sozialnetzwerken zu verbessern.

es.title: Filtrado adaptativo al usuario basado en agentes para comunicación de acoso categorizado
es.summary: Este artículo propone un marco basado en agentes para personalizar filtros de moderación de contenido en función de los niveles de tolerancia individual de los usuarios en diferentes categorías de acoso. A diferencia de las reglas de moderación globales, los agentes adaptativos aprenden de la retroalimentación del usuario para ajustar dinámicamente los umbrales de filtrado, mejorando tanto la precisión como la satisfacción del usuario en las redes sociales en línea.

title: DOVA: Deliberation-First Multi-Agent Orchestration for Autonomous Research Automation
summary: DOVA is a multi-agent LLM platform that uses deliberation-first orchestration to coordinate multiple agents on complex research tasks, featuring explicit meta-reasoning before tool invocation and adaptive token-budget allocation that reduces inference cost by 40-60% while maintaining reasoning depth. The system leverages persistent user models and hybrid collaborative reasoning to improve answer confidence and source coverage.
content:
arXiv:2603.13327v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in tool use, reasoning, and code generation, yet single-agent systems exhibit fundamental limitations when confronted with complex research tasks demanding multi-source synthesis, adversarial verification, and personalized delivery. We present DOVA (Deep Orchestrated Versatile Agent), a multi-agent platform introducing three key innovations: (1) deliberation-first orchestration, where explicit meta-reasoning precedes tool invocation, informed by a persistent user model and entity-aware conversation context; (2) hybrid collaborative reasoning, a composable three-phase pipeline unifying ensemble diversity, blackboard transparency, and iterative refinement; and (3) adaptive multi-tiered thinking, a six-level token-budget allocation scheme that reduces inference cost by 40-60% on simple tasks while preserving deep reasoning capacity. We formalize the core algorithms, present an architectural ablation study across seven system configurations, and analyze the contribution of each component to answer confidence, source coverage, and token efficiency.

zh.title: DOVA：用于自主研究自动化的深思熟虑优先的多代理编排
zh.summary: DOVA是一个多代理LLM平台，采用深思熟虑优先的编排来协调多个代理处理复杂的研究任务，在工具调用前进行显式的元推理，并采用自适应的令牌预算分配，使推理成本降低40-60%，同时保持推理深度。该系统利用持久用户模型和混合协作推理来提高答案置信度和来源覆盖范围。

fr.title: DOVA : Orchestration multi-agents axée sur la délibération pour l'automatisation de la recherche autonome
fr.summary: DOVA est une plateforme LLM multi-agents qui utilise une orchestration axée sur la délibération pour coordonner plusieurs agents sur des tâches de recherche complexes, avec un raisonnement méta-explicite avant l'invocation d'outils et une allocation adaptative du budget de jetons qui réduit les coûts d'inférence de 40 à 60% tout en maintenant la profondeur du raisonnement. Le système s'appuie sur des modèles utilisateurs persistants et un raisonnement collaboratif hybride pour améliorer la confiance dans les réponses et la couverture des sources.

de.title: DOVA: Überlegung-zuerst Multi-Agenten-Orchestrierung für autonome Forschungsautomatisierung
de.summary: DOVA ist eine Multi-Agenten-LLM-Plattform, die Überlegung-zuerst-Orchestrierung verwendet, um mehrere Agenten bei komplexen Forschungsaufgaben zu koordinieren, mit explizitem Meta-Reasoning vor der Tool-Invokation und adaptiver Token-Budget-Zuweisung, die Inferenzkosten um 40-60% senkt und dabei die Reasoning-Tiefe beibehält. Das System nutzt persistente Benutzermodelle und hybrides kooperatives Denken, um die Antwortvertrauensicherheit und Quellenabdeckung zu verbessern.

es.title: DOVA: Orquestación Multi-Agente Centrada en la Deliberación para la Automatización de Investigación Autónoma
es.summary: DOVA es una plataforma LLM multi-agente que utiliza orquestación centrada en la deliberación para coordinar múltiples agentes en tareas de investigación complejas, con razonamiento meta-explícito antes de la invocación de herramientas y asignación adaptativa del presupuesto de tokens que reduce los costos de inferencia en un 40-60% mientras mantiene la profundidad del razonamiento. El sistema aprovecha modelos de usuario persistentes y razonamiento colaborativo híbrido para mejorar la confianza en las respuestas y la cobertura de fuentes.

title: Why Grokking Takes So Long: A First-Principles Theory of Representational Phase Transitions
summary: This paper develops the first quantitative theory explaining why neural networks suddenly generalize long after memorizing training data, showing grokking arises from a norm-driven representational phase transition. The work establishes a precise scaling law for the delay and confirms predictions about inverse scaling with weight decay and learning rate across modular arithmetic and parity tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.13331v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Grokking is the sudden generalization that appears long after a model has perfectly memorized its training data. Although this phenomenon has been widely observed, there is still no quantitative theory explaining the length of the delay between memorization and generalization. Prior work has noted that weight decay plays an important role, but no result derives tight bounds for the delay or explains its scaling behavior.
  We present a first-principles theory showing that grokking arises from a norm-driven representational phase transition in regularized training dynamics. Training first converges to a high-norm memorization solution and only later contracts toward a lower-norm structured representation that generalizes.
  Our main result establishes a scaling law for the delay:
  T_grok - T_mem = Theta((1 / gamma_eff) * log(||theta_mem||^2 / ||theta_post||^2)),
  where gamma_eff is the effective contraction rate of the optimizer (gamma_eff = eta * lambda for SGD and gamma_eff >= eta * lambda for AdamW). The upper bound follows from a discrete Lyapunov contraction argument, and the matching lower bound arises from dynamical constraints of regularized first-order optimization.
  Across 293 training runs spanning modular addition, modular multiplication, and sparse parity tasks, we confirm three predictions: inverse scaling with weight decay, inverse scaling with learning rate, and logarithmic dependence on the norm ratio (R^2 > 0.97). We further find that grokking requires an optimizer that can decouple memorization from contraction: SGD fails under hyperparameters where AdamW reliably groks.
  These results show that grokking is a predictable consequence of norm separation between competing interpolating representations and provide the first quantitative scaling law for the delay of grokking.

zh.title: 为什么Grokking耗时如此之长：表示阶段转变的第一性原理理论
zh.summary: 本文开发了第一个定量理论，解释为什么神经网络在记忆训练数据长时间后突然泛化，表明grokking源于范数驱动的表示阶段转变。该工作为延迟建立了精确的缩放律，并在模运算和奇偶性任务中确认了关于权重衰减和学习率的反向缩放的预测。

fr.title: Pourquoi Grokking prend si longtemps : une théorie des transitions de phase représentationnelles basée sur les principes premiers
fr.summary: Cet article développe la première théorie quantitative expliquant pourquoi les réseaux de neurones se généralisent soudainement longtemps après la mémorisation des données d'entraînement, montrant que le grokking résulte d'une transition de phase représentationnelle basée sur la norme. Le travail établit une loi d'échelle précise pour le délai et confirme les prédictions sur l'échelle inverse avec la décroissance des poids et le taux d'apprentissage sur les tâches d'arithmétique modulaire et de parité.

de.title: Warum Grokking so lange dauert: Eine First-Principles-Theorie der Phasenübergänge in der Darstellung
de.summary: Dieses Papier entwickelt die erste quantitative Theorie, die erklärt, warum neuronale Netze lange nach dem Auswendiglernen von Trainingsdaten plötzlich verallgemeinern, und zeigt, dass Grokking aus einem normgetriebenen Darstellungsphasenübergang resultiert. Die Arbeit etabliert ein präzises Skalierungsgesetz für die Verzögerung und bestätigt Vorhersagen über umgekehrte Skalierung mit Gewichtsverlust und Lernrate über modulare Arithmetik und Parität-Aufgaben.

es.title: Por qué Grokking toma tanto tiempo: Una teoría de primeros principios de transiciones de fase representacionales
es.summary: Este artículo desarrolla la primera teoría cuantitativa que explica por qué las redes neuronales se generalizan repentinamente mucho después de memorizar datos de entrenamiento, mostrando que el grokking surge de una transición de fase representacional impulsada por la norma. El trabajo establece una ley de escalado precisa para el retraso y confirma predicciones sobre escalado inverso con decaimiento de peso y tasa de aprendizaje en tareas de aritmética modular y paridad.

title: DyACE: Dynamic Algorithm Co-evolution for Online Automated Heuristic Design with Large Language Model
summary: DyACE introduces a dynamic algorithm co-evolution framework that adapts heuristics based on real-time search feedback using LLMs as meta-controllers for combinatorial optimization. Unlike static algorithms, DyACE continuously adjusts its logic based on search phase and landscape geometry, significantly outperforming fixed baselines on benchmark problems. The approach demonstrates that effective automated heuristic design requires grounded perception rather than one-size-fits-all algorithms.
content:
arXiv:2603.13344v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The prevailing paradigm in Automated Heuristic Design (AHD) typically relies on the assumption that a single, fixed algorithm can effectively navigate the shifting dynamics of a combinatorial search. This static approach often proves inadequate for Perturbative Heuristics, where the optimal algorithm for escaping local optima depends heavily on the specific search phase. To address this limitation, we reformulate heuristic design as a Non-stationary Bi-level Control problem and introduce DyACE (Dynamic Algorithm Co-evolution). Distinct from standard open-loop solvers, DyACE use a Receding Horizon Control architecture to continuously co-evolve the heuristic logic alongside the solution population. A core element of this framework is the Look-Ahead Rollout Search, which queries the landscape geometry to extract Search Trajectory Features. This sensory feedback allows the Large Language Model (LLM) to function as a grounded meta-controller, prescribing phase-specific interventions tailored to the real-time search status. We validate DyACE on three representative combinatorial optimization benchmarks. The results demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art static baselines, exhibiting superior scalability in high-dimensional search spaces. Furthermore, ablation studies confirm that dynamic adaptation fails without grounded perception, often performing worse than static algorithms. This indicates that DyACE's effectiveness stems from the causal alignment between the synthesized logic and the verified gradients of the optimization landscape.

zh.title: DyACE：用大型语言模型进行在线自动启发式设计的动态算法协进化
zh.summary: DyACE引入了一个动态算法协进化框架，该框架基于使用LLM作为元控制器的实时搜索反馈来调整启发式方法，用于组合优化。与静态算法不同，DyACE根据搜索阶段和景观几何形状不断调整其逻辑，在基准问题上的性能明显优于固定基线。该方法表明，有效的自动启发式设计需要基于感知而不是一刀切的算法。

fr.title: DyACE : Co-évolution d'algorithme dynamique pour la conception heuristique automatisée en ligne avec un grand modèle de langage
fr.summary: DyACE introduit un cadre de co-évolution d'algorithmes dynamiques qui adapte les heuristiques en fonction des retours de recherche en temps réel en utilisant les LLM comme méta-contrôleurs pour l'optimisation combinatoire. Contrairement aux algorithmes statiques, DyACE ajuste continuellement sa logique en fonction de la phase de recherche et de la géométrie du paysage, surpassant considérablement les baselines fixes sur les problèmes de référence. L'approche démontre que la conception heuristique automatisée efficace nécessite une perception fondée plutôt que des algorithmes universels.

de.title: DyACE: Dynamische Algorithmus-Koevolution für das Online-Design automatisierter Heuristiken mit großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: DyACE führt ein Framework für dynamische Algorithmus-Koevolution ein, das Heuristiken basierend auf Echtzeit-Suchrückmeldungen anpasst, wobei LLMs als Metacontroller für kombinatorische Optimierung verwendet werden. Im Gegensatz zu statischen Algorithmen passt DyACE seine Logik kontinuierlich basierend auf der Suchphase und der Landschaftsgeometrie an und übertrifft feste Baselines bei Benchmark-Problemen erheblich. Der Ansatz zeigt, dass wirksames automatisiertes Heuristic-Design auf fundierter Wahrnehmung basieren muss, nicht auf universellen Algorithmen.

es.title: DyACE: Co-evolución dinámica de algoritmos para el diseño automatizado de heurísticas en línea con modelos de lenguaje grandes
es.summary: DyACE introduce un marco de co-evolución dinámica de algoritmos que adapta las heurísticas basándose en retroalimentación de búsqueda en tiempo real utilizando LLM como metacontroladores para optimización combinatoria. A diferencia de los algoritmos estáticos, DyACE ajusta continuamente su lógica en función de la fase de búsqueda y la geometría del paisaje, superando significativamente las líneas de base fijas en problemas de referencia. El enfoque demuestra que el diseño automático de heurísticas efectivo requiere percepción fundamentada en lugar de algoritmos universales.

title: AutoTool: Automatic Scaling of Tool-Use Capabilities in RL via Decoupled Entropy Constraints
summary: The paper proposes a training paradigm combining warm-up supervised fine-tuning with entropy-constrained reinforcement learning to enable models to automatically scale reasoning effort based on problem complexity. Experiments achieve 9.8% accuracy improvements while reducing computational overhead by approximately 81%, addressing inefficiency in models that overthink simpler problems. The work highlights that multi-scale reasoning control is critical for practical tool-use at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.13348v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tool use represents a critical capability for AI agents, with recent advances focusing on leveraging reinforcement learning (RL) to scale up the explicit reasoning process to achieve better performance. However, there are some key challenges for tool use in current RL-based scaling approaches: (a) direct RL training often struggles to scale up thinking length sufficiently to solve complex problems, and (b) scaled-up models tend to overthink simpler problems, resulting in substantial token inefficiency. To address these challenges, we propose a novel training paradigm that first employs warm-up supervised fine-tuning to help models distinguish between simple and complex problems, followed by RL that enable models to automatically determine appropriate reasoning trajectories. Furthermore, to tackle the issue of automatic thinking-length scaling, we discover that entropy-based optimization objectives effectively maintain model diversity while successfully unlocking the model's scaling capabilities. Based on this insight, we introduce an entropy-based long-short reasoning fusion RL strategy. Our experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate that model successfully achieves auto-scaling for efficient tool use, achieving significant 9.8\% accuracy improvements while reducing computational overhead by \textasciitilde81\%.

zh.title: AutoTool：通过解耦熵约束在强化学习中自动扩展工具使用能力
zh.summary: 该论文提出了一个结合热启动监督微调和熵约束强化学习的训练范式，使模型能够根据问题复杂性自动扩展推理工作量。实验实现了9.8%的准确率改进，同时将计算开销减少了约81%，解决了模型在较简单问题上过度思考的低效问题。该工作强调，多尺度推理控制对于大规模工具使用的实际应用至关重要。

fr.title: AutoTool : Mise à l'échelle automatique des capacités d'utilisation d'outils en RL via des contraintes d'entropie découplées
fr.summary: L'article propose un paradigme d'entraînement combinant un affinage supervisé de préchauffage avec un apprentissage par renforcement contraint en entropie pour permettre aux modèles de mettre automatiquement à l'échelle l'effort de raisonnement en fonction de la complexité du problème. Les expériences réalisent des améliorations de précision de 9,8% tout en réduisant les frais généraux de calcul d'environ 81%, résolvant l'inefficacité des modèles qui surréfléchissent à des problèmes plus simples. L'article souligne que le contrôle du raisonnement à plusieurs échelles est essentiel pour l'utilisation pratique des outils à grande échelle.

de.title: AutoTool: Automatische Skalierung von Tool-Use-Fähigkeiten in RL durch entkoppelte Entropie-Beschränkungen
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt ein Trainingsparadigma vor, das betreute Vorwärmungs-Feinabstimmung mit entropie-beschränktem Reinforcement Learning kombiniert, um Modellen automatisch Denkanstrengungen basierend auf Problemkomplexität zu skalieren. Experimente erreichen Genauigkeitsverbesserungen von 9,8% und reduzieren gleichzeitig den Rechenaufwand um etwa 81%, was die Ineffizienz von Modellen behebt, die über einfachere Probleme überdenken. Die Arbeit betont, dass die Kontrolle des mehrschaligen Denkens für praktische Tool-Use-Anwendungen in großem Maßstab kritisch ist.

es.title: AutoTool: Escalado automático de capacidades de uso de herramientas en RL mediante restricciones de entropía desacopladas
es.summary: El artículo propone un paradigma de entrenamiento que combina ajuste fino supervisado de calentamiento con aprendizaje por refuerzo restringido por entropía para permitir que los modelos escalen automáticamente el esfuerzo de razonamiento en función de la complejidad del problema. Los experimentos logran mejoras de precisión del 9,8% mientras reducen la sobrecarga computacional en aproximadamente un 81%, abordando la ineficiencia de los modelos que piensan demasiado en problemas más simples. El trabajo destaca que el control de razonamiento multiescala es crítico para el uso práctico de herramientas a escala.

title: Prompt Complexity Dilutes Structured Reasoning: A Follow-Up Study on the Car Wash Problem
summary: STAR reasoning achieves 100% accuracy in isolation but degrades to 0-30% when embedded in complex production prompts with competing instructions, due to conflicting directives that force conclusion-first output. The mechanism reverses the reason-then-conclude logic that makes STAR effective, revealing a fundamental gap between isolated prompt testing and real-world deployment. The findings demonstrate that structured reasoning frameworks require careful prompt engineering and cannot be assumed to transfer across contexts.
content:
arXiv:2603.13351v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In a previous study [Jo, 2026], STAR reasoning (Situation, Task, Action, Result) raised car wash problem accuracy from 0% to 85% on Claude Sonnet 4.5, and to 100% with
  additional prompt layers. This follow-up asks: does STAR maintain its effectiveness in a production system prompt?
  We tested STAR inside InterviewMate's 60+ line production prompt, which had evolved through iterative additions of style guidelines, format instructions, and profile
  features. Three conditions, 20 trials each, on Claude Sonnet 4.6: (A) production prompt with Anthropic profile, (B) production prompt with default profile, (C) original
  STAR-only prompt. C scored 100% (verified at n=100). A and B scored 0% and 30%.
  Prompt complexity dilutes structured reasoning. STAR achieves 100% in isolation but degrades to 0-30% when surrounded by competing instructions. The mechanism: directives
  like "Lead with specifics" force conclusion-first output, reversing the reason-then-conclude order that makes STAR effective. In one case, the model output "Short answer:
  Walk." then executed STAR reasoning that correctly identified the constraint -- proving the model could reason correctly but had already committed to the wrong answer.
  Cross-model comparison shows STAR-only improved from 85% (Sonnet 4.5) to 100% (Sonnet 4.6) without prompt changes, suggesting model upgrades amplify structured reasoning
  in isolation.
  These results imply structured reasoning frameworks should not be assumed to transfer from isolated testing to complex prompt environments. The order in which a model
  reasons and concludes is a first-class design variable.

zh.title: 提示复杂性削弱结构化推理：关于洗车问题的后续研究
zh.summary: STAR推理在隔离状态下实现100%的准确率，但当嵌入到具有相互竞争指令的复杂生产提示中时会降至0-30%，因为冲突指令强制执行先下结论的输出。该机制反转了使STAR有效的先推理后结论逻辑，揭示了隔离提示测试和实际部署之间的根本差距。这些发现表明，结构化推理框架需要仔细的提示工程，不能假设在不同环境中转移。

fr.title: La complexité des invites dilue le raisonnement structuré : une étude de suivi sur le problème du lavage de voitures
fr.summary: Le raisonnement STAR atteint une précision de 100% en isolation, mais se dégrade à 0-30% lorsqu'il est intégré dans des invites de production complexes avec des instructions concurrentes, en raison de directives conflictuelles qui forcent une sortie conclusion-première. Le mécanisme inverse la logique raison-puis-conclure qui rend STAR efficace, révélant un écart fondamental entre les tests de suggestions isolées et le déploiement dans le monde réel. Les résultats démontrent que les cadres de raisonnement structuré nécessitent une ingénierie soigneuse des suggestions et ne peuvent pas être supposés être transférables entre les contextes.

de.title: Prompt-Komplexität schwächt strukturiertes Denken: Eine Folgestudie zum Car-Wash-Problem
de.summary: STAR-Denken erreicht 100% Genauigkeit in Isolation, verschlechtert sich aber auf 0-30%, wenn es in komplexe Produktions-Prompts mit konkurrierenden Anweisungen eingebettet wird, da widersprüchliche Direktiven erzwingen, dass die Schlussfolgerung zuerst ausgegeben wird. Der Mechanismus kehrt die Denk-dann-Schließen-Logik um, die STAR wirksam macht, und offenbart eine grundlegende Lücke zwischen isoliertem Prompt-Testen und Einsatz in der Praxis. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass strukturierte Denkrahmen sorgfältige Prompt-Engineering erfordern und nicht davon ausgegangen werden kann, dass sie zwischen Kontexten übertragbar sind.

es.title: La complejidad de los indicadores diluye el razonamiento estructurado: un estudio de seguimiento del problema del lavado de autos
es.summary: El razonamiento STAR logra una precisión del 100% en aislamiento, pero se degrada a 0-30% cuando se incrusta en indicadores complejos de producción con instrucciones en competencia, debido a directivas conflictivas que fuerzan una salida conclusión-primero. El mecanismo invierte la lógica razón-luego-conclusión que hace que STAR sea efectivo, revelando una brecha fundamental entre las pruebas de indicadores aislados y el despliegue en el mundo real. Los hallazgos demuestran que los marcos de razonamiento estructurado requieren una ingeniería de indicadores cuidadosa y no se puede asumir que se transfieran entre contextos.

title: Optimizing LLM Annotation of Classroom Discourse through Multi-Agent Orchestration
summary: This paper presents a hierarchical multi-agent framework for annotating classroom discourse through three stages—initial annotation, self-verification, and disagreement adjudication—mirroring human expert workflows. Rather than treating annotation as a single prediction task, the approach improves reliability for high-stakes educational constructs requiring contextual pedagogical judgment. Experiments demonstrate the framework's effectiveness at balancing cost and validity in educational data annotation.
content:
arXiv:2603.13353v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly positioned as scalable tools for annotating educational data, including classroom discourse, interaction logs, and qualitative learning artifacts. Their ability to rapidly summarize instructional interactions and assign rubric-aligned labels has fueled optimism about reducing the cost and time associated with expert human annotation. However, growing evidence suggests that single-pass LLM outputs remain unreliable for high-stakes educational constructs that require contextual, pedagogical, or normative judgment, such as instructional intent or discourse moves. This tension between scale and validity sits at the core of contemporary education data science. In this work, we present and empirically evaluate a hierarchical, cost-aware orchestration framework for LLM-based annotation that improves reliability while explicitly modeling computational tradeoffs. Rather than treating annotation as a one-shot prediction problem, we conceptualize it as a multi-stage epistemic process comprising (1) an unverified single-pass annotation stage, in which models independently assign labels based on the rubric; (2) a self-verification stage, in which each model audits its own output against rubric definitions and revises its label if inconsistencies are detected; and (3) a disagreement-centric adjudication stage, in which an independent adjudicator model examines the verified labels and justifications and determines a final label in accordance with the rubric. This structure mirrors established human annotation workflows in educational research, where initial coding is followed by self-checking and expert resolution of disagreements.

zh.title: 通过多智能体编排优化教室话语的大语言模型标注
zh.summary: 本论文提出了一个分层多智能体框架，用于通过三个阶段对教室话语进行标注——初始标注、自验证和分歧仲裁——镜像人类专家工作流程。该方法不是将标注视为单一预测任务，而是改进了需要上下文教学判断的高风险教育构建的可靠性。实验证明了该框架在平衡教育数据标注成本和有效性方面的有效性。

fr.title: Optimisation de l'annotation par LLM du discours en classe grâce à l'orchestration multi-agents
fr.summary: Cet article présente un cadre multi-agents hiérarchique pour annoter le discours en classe en trois étapes—annotation initiale, auto-vérification et arbitrage des désaccords—reflétant les flux de travail des experts humains. Plutôt que de traiter l'annotation comme une tâche de prédiction unique, l'approche améliore la fiabilité des construits éducatifs à enjeux élevés nécessitant un jugement pédagogique contextuel. Les expériences démontrent l'efficacité du cadre à équilibrer le coût et la validité dans l'annotation des données éducatives.

de.title: Optimierung der LLM-Annotation von Klassenzimmerdiskursen durch Multi-Agent-Orchestrierung
de.summary: In diesem Papier wird ein hierarchisches Multi-Agent-Framework zur Annotation von Klassenzimmerdiskursen in drei Phasen vorgestellt – Initialannotation, Selbstverifikation und Schiedsverfahren bei Meinungsverschiedenheiten – das menschliche Fachleute-Arbeitsabläufe widerspiegelt. Anstatt die Anmerkung als einzelne Vorhersageaufgabe zu behandeln, verbessert der Ansatz die Zuverlässigkeit für hochriskante Bildungskonstrukte, die kontextbezogenes pädagogisches Urteilsvermögen erfordern. Experimente demonstrieren die Effektivität des Rahmens beim Ausgleich von Kosten und Gültigkeit in der Annotation von Bildungsdaten.

es.title: Optimización de la anotación de LLM del discurso en el aula mediante orquestación multiagente
es.summary: Este documento presenta un marco jerárquico multiagente para anotar el discurso en el aula en tres etapas: anotación inicial, auto-verificación y arbitraje de desacuerdos, espejando flujos de trabajo de expertos humanos. En lugar de tratar la anotación como una única tarea de predicción, el enfoque mejora la confiabilidad de construcciones educativas de alto riesgo que requieren juicio pedagógico contextual. Los experimentos demuestran la efectividad del marco para equilibrar el costo y la validez en la anotación de datos educativos.

title: Learning When to Trust in Contextual Bandits
summary: Researchers identify "Contextual Sycophancy," where feedback sources are truthful in benign contexts but strategically biased in critical ones, and propose CESA-LinUCB to learn per-evaluator trust boundaries. The algorithm achieves sublinear regret against contextual adversaries and recovers ground truth even when no evaluator is globally reliable. This work extends robust reinforcement learning to more realistic evaluation scenarios where trustworthiness is context-dependent.
content:
arXiv:2603.13356v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Standard approaches to Robust Reinforcement Learning assume that feedback sources are either globally trustworthy or globally adversarial. In this paper, we challenge this assumption and we identify a more subtle failure mode. We term this mode as Contextual Sycophancy, where evaluators are truthful in benign contexts but strategically biased in critical ones. We prove that standard robust methods fail in this setting, suffering from Contextual Objective Decoupling. To address this, we propose CESA-LinUCB, which learns a high-dimensional Trust Boundary for each evaluator. We prove that CESA-LinUCB achieves sublinear regret $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ against contextual adversaries, recovering the ground truth even when no evaluator is globally reliable.

zh.title: 学习在上下文赌博机中何时信任
